classification_results.rationale: BREADTH=6 (affects 6 L1 categories); CR=0.25 < 0.50; HHI=0.18 < 0.25; Tests: Relevance=DRIVER, Distinction=DRIVER, Materiality=DRIVER
BREADTH=4 (affects 4 L1 categories); CR=0.72 ≥ 0.50; HHI=0.55 ≥ 0.25; Tests: Relevance=SUB-RISK, Distinction=SUB-RISK, Materiality=SUB-RISK; Factorization parent: Operational Risk
BREADTH=4 (affects 4 L1 categories); CR=0.35 < 0.50; HHI=0.28 ≥ 0.25; Tests: Relevance=DRIVER, Distinction=DRIVER, Materiality=SUB-RISK; Majority rule: 2/3 DRIVER
BREADTH=6 (affects all 6 L1 categories); CR=0.35 < 0.50; HHI=0.23 < 0.25; Tests: Relevance=DRIVER, Distinction=DRIVER, Materiality=DRIVER; Unanimous DRIVER classification

_validation.description: Expected output for manuscript case studies

alignment.geopolitical_risk: DRIVER (unanimous) - consistent with cross-cutting treatment in regulatory practice
alignment.model_risk: SUB-RISK under Operational (unanimous) - consistent with Basel SR 11-7
alignment.climate_risk: DRIVER (2/3 majority) - Materiality test indicates SUB-RISK due to HHI=0.275, but Relevance and Distinction tests override; consistent with NGFS/TCFD cross-cutting treatment
alignment.digital_assets_risk: DRIVER (unanimous) - consistent with emerging regulatory treatment (OCC, SEC, Basel BCBS) recognizing cross-cutting nature spanning market volatility, operational/custody risks, and counterparty exposures

_validation.note: Climate Risk demonstrates the majority-rule decision logic: despite meeting the HHI threshold (0.275 ≥ 0.25), the candidate is classified as DRIVER because 2 of 3 tests favor that classification. Digital Assets Risk shows unanimous DRIVER classification with dispersed impact across all six L1 categories.
